sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Diastasis recti screening looks at abdominal wall function, contour change, and associated disability rather than focusing only on gap width.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation
sections.content:
- Visible doming or bulging during effort
- Feeling of reduced abdominal control or support
- Difficulty with transfers, lifting, or return to exercise after pregnancy
- Possible overlap with back pain or pelvic floor symptoms

sections.title: Considerations
sections.content:
- Escalate for suspected hernia, severe pain, wound concerns, or unusual abdominal symptoms
- A visible gap alone does not necessarily equal dysfunction
- Advice should avoid fear-based messaging about permanent damage from normal movement

sections.title: Management - Primary
sections.content:
- Screen whether abdominal symptoms are affecting function and whether any red flags are present
- Encourage supported rolling, bracing awareness, and graded return to load within tolerance
- Escalate if hernia or wound concerns are suspected

sections.title: Management - Advanced
sections.content:
- Assess abdominal wall tension, symptom provocation, transfer control, breathing pattern, and load tolerance
- Build graded trunk and whole-body conditioning rather than isolated cosmetic-focused exercises alone
- Refer for pelvic health, obstetric, or surgical review when hernia, severe dysfunction, or unresolved concern persists

sections.title: Additional Notes
sections.content: - Function, symptom behaviour, and confidence matter more than a single gap measurement
